title: symphytum × ullepitschii
intro: Symphytum × ullepitschii, also known as Ullmann's comfrey, is a hybrid plant of the comfrey species, Symphtum officinale. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, sprains, and bone fractures, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce pain. Comfrey root and leaves are often used in herbal preparations, including teas, salves, and tinctures, to leverage its health benefits and aid in the recovery of injured tissues.
